summary: Drew DeVault signal-boosts an article from Lichess explaining why the chess platform will always remain free. The post is extremely brief, consisting of just a link recommendation with no original analysis. It reflects DeVault's ongoing advocacy for free, non-commercial software projects. By sharing this, he implicitly endorses Lichess as a model for how internet services should operate.
key_points: Lichess is held up as an exemplary free software project worth promoting
The post is a pure signal-boost with no original commentary, indicating DeVault considers the Lichess article self-evidently aligned with his values
Implicitly contrasts Lichess's model against commercial chess platforms like Chess.com
money_quotes: Signal-boosting this excellent article from Lichess: Why Lichess will always be free.
key_insight: Lichess represents the kind of free, non-commercial internet service that DeVault believes should be the norm rather than the exception.
